message:
fix(release): re-pin asupersync to 310ff61f; bump to v0.2.45

v0.2.44 dist pinned to db186126 which turned out to have io_uring.rs
trait impl visibility errors on Linux (17× E0449 / E0407 on
`pub fn register_buffer_pool` inside a `impl Reactor for IoUringReactor`
block; trait impls forbid visibility qualifiers). macOS and Windows
builds passed because the offending module is cfg-gated to
`target_os = "linux", feature = "io-uring"`.

Re-pin all three clones to 310ff61f (`fix(cancel): unbreak lib build
for downstream crates`), a proven-clean earlier commit that CASS
already pins via Cargo.lock. Verified locally with `cargo check --lib`
against a fresh 310ff61f worktree — finishes with zero errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -19,7 +19,7 @@ jobs:
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensearch ../frankensearch
           # asupersync main is transiently broken (trait-breaking API churn upstream); pin to last SHA known-good for mcp_agent_mail_rust builds. Unpin when upstream main cleanly compiles.
           git clone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync ../asupersync
-          git -C ../asupersync checkout --quiet db186126b40d9d3028be731ff1bfea47614e2b1b
+          git -C ../asupersync checkout --quiet 310ff61f9b7073ef7a702c64ab3f56dc8962446f
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/sqlmodel_rust ../sqlmodel_rust
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite ../frankensqlite
           git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankentui ../frankentui
